mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 0.25% signal zero lower bound monetary accommodation. This extreme easing compresses equity risk premiums and reduces discount rates on corporate cash flows, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.14% as of 2015-08-15 indicates near-zero policy rate
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 40% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any rolling 2-year window during ZLB periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll employment growth above 200,000 persons signals robust labor market momentum and consumer income expansion. This drives aggregate demand and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher (t-test p > 0.05) during periods of PAYEMS growth > 200k month-over-month versus baseline, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts measured by HOUST decline by more than 10% month-over-month, signaling deterioration in residential investment and forward-looking construction demand. This contraction in a key demand component predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,206 thousand units as of 2015-07-15
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances following HOUST declines > 10%, or Rank IC > -0.03 (fails to show negative correlation) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index INDPRO and inflation-adjusted retail sales RSAFS move in opposite directions: INDPRO declining while RSAFS remains flat or rising indicates supply-side weakness with demand resilience. This supply-demand imbalance compresses profit margins and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change < -0.5% AND RSAFS month-over-month change > -0.2%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 447,715 million dollars as of 2015-08-15
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) when the interaction condition is triggered, or the joint signal produces Rank IC < 0.02 in absolute value over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month rolling window, signaling labor market deterioration and forward-looking recession risk. This deterioration predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as equity investors reprice earnings expectations downward.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) following UNRATE increases > 0.3pp, or Rank IC > -0.04 (fails to show meaningful negative correlation) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation measured by CPIAUCSL accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while FEDFUNDS remains below 1.0%, indicating real rates are negative and monetary policy is accommodative. This real rate compression drives equity valuations higher, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) when the interaction condition is triggered, or the joint signal produces Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth GDPC1 decelerates to below 2.0% annualized rate, signaling late-cycle demand exhaustion. This growth deceleration predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as investors anticipate earnings downgrades and recession risk.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 16,324.3 billion chained 2017 dollars as of 2015-04-15
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) when GDPC1 growth < 2.0%, or Rank IC > -0.05 (fails to show meaningful negative correlation) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production INDPRO accelerates above 0.5% month-over-month while unemployment UNRATE remains stable or declines, indicating supply-side capacity expansion with tight labor markets. This supply-side strength with labor tightness supports profit margins and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change > 0.5% AND UNRATE month-over-month change <= 0%
regime_hint: supply-driven expansion with labor market tightness
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) when the interaction condition is triggered, or the joint signal produces Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
